contributor: university of illinois at urbana-champaign library, urbana, il
description: What Lies Behind the London Coup By R PALME DUTT London THE lawless bandit coup of the Lon don tory government on the Soviet Delegation offices is the most flag rant act yet of violent aggression in trie unproclaimed war which the British government is conducting against the Soviet Union Chamber lain no longer hides behind his con federate brigands and murderers the ChangTsoLins and Pilsudskis but comes into the open himself com plete with burglars tools and modern safebreaking paraphernalia as the Chief Brigand and leader of the cam paign With this act the lawless ag gression of the British government is openly proclaimed to the world THIS coup is an attempt to force a situation of war by violent and desperate measures in face of the continued pacific attitude of the Sov iet Union and of the growingly criti cal character of the situation at home and abroad It is a continuation of the policy already initiated with the note to the Soviet Union in February and continued with the diplomatic out rages arrests and murders at Peking Tientsin and Shanghai It is part of the same policy that is training the naval guns on Hankow to smash
